name: Domains of life in modern classification
question_text: Which three domains are recognised in the modern three-domain system of biological classification?
general_feedback: Carl Woese's three-domain system groups cellular life into Bacteria, Archaea, and Eukarya, reflecting deep rRNA-based differences. Viruses are not included because they are not cellular.
correct_feedback: Correct — The three-domain system introduced by Woese, based on ribosomal RNA phylogeny.

answers.feedback: The three-domain system introduced by Woese, based on ribosomal RNA phylogeny.
Those are kingdoms within Eukarya, not domains.
Viruses are not classified as a domain of cellular life in the three-domain system.
These are older or kingdom-level names and do not form the modern three-domain system.
